chain_edges.notes: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 is a concrete CVSS 8+ LLM-integration CVE instance feeding the broader Patch-Tuesday-regular thesis into GGUF-specific gating.
Signed model-cards and chat_template verification become artifacts a registry standard would distribute alongside agent skills and permissions.
Native 1-bit GGUF derivatives (Bonsai-8B-gguf) expand the GGUF artifact surface that hubs must then gate behind signed model-cards.

relations.notes: GGUF SSTI gating is a narrower, GGUF-specific instance of the broader LLM-integration CVE category becoming a Patch Tuesday regular.
Both name new agent/model supply-chain attack categories (GGUF artifacts vs CI secret exfiltration) without overlapping outcome spaces.
Both center on GGUF artifacts but address independent facets: native 1-bit training recipe versus model-load SSTI gating.
Local-first coding agents rely on GGUF distribution; supply-chain gating is a parallel prerequisite, not a stronger relation.
Agent Registry distributes agent artifacts; model-card/chat_template signing is a parallel registry-shaped outcome for model artifacts.
